diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Amenorrhoea in a reproductive-age woman with a positive test strongly supports pregnancy.
Urine beta-hCG detects pregnancy but not location — patient-reported, unverified, does not confirm intrauterine implantation.
A positive test is equally consistent with ectopic — location must be confirmed by ultrasound.

symptoms.symptom_name: Early pregnancy symptoms — morning nausea and breast tenderness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Congruent early pregnancy symptoms increase pre-test probability of a true positive.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute racing heart and fast breathing after shocking news, settling with de-escalation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sudden autonomic arousal triggered by acute psychological stress, easing with slowed breathing.
No chest pain, no radiation, no exertional trigger, young female — panic far more likely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of severe unilateral lower abdominal / pelvic pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reduces current likelihood of symptomatic/ruptured ectopic but does not exclude it before imaging.
No bleeding lowers current concern for ectopic/miscarriage but does not exclude an evolving ectopic.
Absence of referred diaphragmatic pain argues against significant intra-abdominal bleeding at present.

symptoms.symptom_name: Soft, non-tender abdomen in all four quadrants; no palpable masses

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician exam finding (sealed) — reassuring for peritonism now; the AI cannot palpate and must not claim this finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Vital signs BP 140/88, HR 110, RR 22, SpO2 100%, afebrile

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tachycardia/tachypnoea/mild hypertension congruent with panic; normal SpO2 and afebrile argue against a physiological emergency. Clinician-measured — sealed.
Haemodynamic stability argues against current significant intra-abdominal haemorrhage.

symptoms.symptom_name: Quantitative serum beta-hCG (appropriately elevated for gestational age)

investigation_details.expected_result: Elevated, appropriate for ~5 weeks gestation; serial values inform viability and location work-up
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Cannot quantify or trend the pregnancy or support ectopic exclusion.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms and quantifies the pregnancy — hospital/pathology test, never available in real time via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Early dating ultrasound showing intrauterine gestational sac

investigation_details.expected_result: Intrauterine gestational sac confirming location and dating
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Pregnancy location remains unconfirmed and ectopic cannot be excluded.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Only imaging demonstrating an intrauterine sac reliably excludes ectopic location.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms and quantifies the pregnancy and, with ultrasound, supports assessment of location and viability.
Baseline early-pregnancy investigation; identifies Rh status relevant to any future bleeding/anti-D decisions.
Confirms intrauterine location and dates the pregnancy; the definitive test to exclude ectopic.
No chest pain, radiation or exertional features; symptoms are congruent with panic — cardiac work-up is not indicated and would reinforce an over-medicalised over-triage.
